description: A dissociative anaesthetic and analgesic, also used at lower doses for treatment-resistant depression and misused recreationally ('Special K'). It works mainly by blocking NMDA glutamate receptors.

halfLife.conditions: Terminal (beta-phase) elimination half-life ~2.5 h (FDA Ketalar label; Clements 1982 gives 186 min = 3.1 h). This is the terminal slope carried for the caption/provenance rows; the engine recomputes beta from the disposition2c micro-parameters (round-trips to ~2.5 h). Distinct from the fast distribution (alpha) phase - see disposition2c and notes.

vd.conditions: Steady-state volume of distribution Vss ~200 L (~2.86 L/kg) for the 70 kg reference subject; reported ~200-252 L across sources. The derived Vc + Vp = 1.0 + 1.86 = 2.86 L/kg reproduces this by construction (Vp is derived as Vss - Vc). Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time.

clearance.conditions: Total plasma clearance ~79 L/h for a 70 kg adult (1.13 L/h/kg), the weighted-mean adult value; Mion 2013's stated range 'ketamine elimination clearance is high (1000-1600 ml/min or 12-20 ml/min/kg)' brackets it (1.13 L/h/kg = 18.8 mL/min/kg); this range was previously mis-attributed to the FDA label, which states no clearance at all. Ketamine clearance is high and hepatic-blood-flow-limited (see notes). Sets AUC = Dose/CL.

centralVd.conditions: Central (initial distribution) volume Vc ~70 L (~1.0 L/kg). Mion 2013 states it directly: 'Central compartment volume is about 70 l'. RE-ATTRIBUTED 2026-07-20: this value previously cited ketalar_label, which on re-reading states NO volume of distribution and no clearance anywhere - only the alpha (10-15 min) and beta (2.5 h) half-lives. The number was right and the citation was not; the corroborating adult estimate is Abuhelwa 2022's Vc 79.3 L/70kg. Sets the immediate post-bolus concentration C(0) = Dose/Vc. Softer than the terminal disposition (as for any 2-comp compound the Vss cross-check constrains CL/beta, not Vc), so the distribution-phase peak amplitude is somewhat illustrative - see the IM magnitude check in notes, which is the first observable test this volume has ever faced.

interCompartmentalClearance.conditions: DERIVED, not reported directly: Q = k12.Vc, computed offline (diazepam-style) from CL, Vc, Vss and the terminal beta so the engine round-trips both the labelled distribution half-life (alpha t1/2 ~10-15 min) and the terminal (beta t1/2 ~2.5 h). sourceRef names the primary structural PK source; the full arithmetic is in notes. ~91 L/h at 70 kg.

peripheralVd.conditions: DERIVED, not reported directly: Vp = Vss - Vc = 2.86 - 1.0 = 1.86 L/kg, with the k12/k21 split fixed by Vp/Vc. sourceRef names the primary structural PK source; full arithmetic in notes. ~130 L at 70 kg.

F.conditions: Bioavailability of an intramuscular dose: absorption completeness ONLY - an injection drains to the systemic circulation without crossing gut wall or portal liver, so unlike the oral F this carries no first-pass term. That distinction is the route's teaching content: ketamine's oral F is ~17% (Clements 1982) against ~64% IM, and the ~4x gap IS first pass. VALUE: Abuhelwa 2022 (population PK in 21 treatment-refractory depressed adults, 0.1-0.5 mg/kg, 'estimated ketamine bioavailability after i.m. and s.c. was ~ 64%'). RANGE spans the whole reported adult+paediatric spread, which Ananyev 2026 tabulates as 41.1-93.0% and explicitly declines to resolve ('the wide range of reported bioavailability values (41.1-93.0%) highlights the challenges in interpreting these results'): the low end is Hornik 2018 (critically ill children, some on ECMO), the high end Clements/Grant 1982 (healthy adults, IM alone). WHY THE MIDDLE VALUE AND NOT CLEMENTS' 93%, given Clements is this file's own structural source: chosen on source characteristics FIRST - Abuhelwa is the only adult source that fits IM data with a full popPK parameter set - and only then magnitude-checked. At F = 0.93 the built curve peaks at 524 ng/mL against a reported ~297 at 1.0 mg/kg (1.76x); at 0.644 the engine gives 182 / 363 / 2178 ng/mL at 0.5 / 1.0 / 6.0 mg/kg against reported 189 / 297 / 1970 - ratios 0.96x / 1.22x / 1.11x, and ~1.1x against the review's own linear trend of ~328 ng/mL per mg/kg (their 1.0 mg/kg point is the low outlier in their own series, not ours). The value was not tuned to the peak; the residual is left standing rather than dialled out. CAVEAT KEPT IN VIEW: Abuhelwa's 64% is estimated for IM and SC COMBINED ('with indistinguishable first-order absorption rate constants'), in patients at subanaesthetic doses - it is not an IM-alone healthy-volunteer number.

ka.conditions: Adult first-order absorption rate constant for the intramuscular depot, 0.109 1/min = 6.54 /h (absorption half-life ~6.4 min). Stated explicitly rather than inverted from a Tmax, because the review reports ka directly; that leaves Tmax free to serve as an independent check, and it passes - the engine's peak lands at ~15 min against the review's reported 10-30 min range and its ~15 min at 1.0 mg/kg. Well clear of flip-flop: ka 6.54/h sits 24x above the terminal beta (0.277/h) and 2.3x above the distribution alpha (2.86/h), so absorption is not rate-limiting and is not close enough to alpha to strain the near-equal-rates guard.

metabolites.name: Norketamine (N-desmethylketamine)
metabolites.description: The active metabolite of ketamine, roughly a third as potent. It is cleared more slowly than the parent, so it lingers and prolongs the effect.

fractionFormed.conditions: ~80% of a ketamine dose is metabolised (N-demethylated, primarily by CYP2B6 and CYP3A4) to the active metabolite norketamine - a single citable fraction (unlike the genotype-bimodal fm that blocks procainamide->NAPA). Point value 80% (range 70-90). Onward hydroxylation to hydroxynorketamine / dehydronorketamine is not modelled.

vd.conditions: DERIVED to be consistent with norketamine's own clearance and half-life: Vd_m = CL_m / k_m = 13.5 L/h / (ln2 / 5 h) ~= 97 L ~= 1.39 L/kg, from the norketamine elimination clearance CLM ~13.5 L/h/70 kg (Kamp 2020 model-based analysis) and t1/2 ~5 h. This makes norketamine exposure effectively clearance-defined (AUC_m = fm.Dose/CL_m); it is NOT an independently measured volume.

halfLife.conditions: Norketamine plasma half-life ~5 h in humans (FDA Ketalar label / Mion 2013). Longer than the parent ketamine's ~2.5 h terminal, so norketamine is ELIMINATION-rate-limited: its own slower disposition governs the terminal decline (the engine's terminal rate = min(k_parent, k_metabolite) reproduces this), and it 'persists more than 5 h after administration.'

metabolites.notes: Norketamine (N-desmethylketamine) is the major circulating metabolite of ketamine and is pharmacologically ACTIVE - about 1/3 as potent as ketamine at the NMDA receptor (FDA label). It is COMPUTED end-to-end by the metabolite engine off the two-compartment parent's alpha/beta modes and DRAWN as a dashed line for both IV routes. Because its own half-life (~5 h) exceeds the parent ketamine's terminal (~2.5 h), it is ELIMINATION-rate-limited: it accumulates and outlives the parent (the classic active-long-lived-metabolite story, compare oxypurinol). TIMING CAVEAT: the FDA label observes norketamine peaking ~30 min after a bolus (rapid formation while ketamine is highest during the alpha-distribution phase), whereas the single-compartment systemic-formation model peaks LATER (~3.3 h) because, with a slow (~5 h) metabolite elimination, most norketamine forms from the sustained beta-phase parent (which carries ~2/3 of the formation AUC) and accumulates - a one-compartment metabolite off a fast-alpha 2-comp parent cannot localise the early peak. The magnitude (~0.4 mcg/mL for a 100 mg bolus) and the long active tail are faithful; only the peak timing runs late (the allopurinol->oxypurinol timing-caveat posture). fm ~80% is directly citable, and the metabolite Vd is derived from its clearance and half-life (nordiazepam posture).

ketalar_label.title: KETALAR (ketamine hydrochloride) injection, CIII - FDA/DailyMed Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology (biphasic: alpha/distribution phase ~45 min, half-life 10-15 min = the anesthetic effect; beta phase terminal half-life 2.5 h; N-dealkylation to active metabolite norketamine by CYP2B6/CYP3A4; norketamine ~1/3 as active as ketamine; ~80% metabolised to norketamine; norketamine plasma half-life ~5 h)

clements_1982.title: Clements JA, Nimmo WS, Grant IS. Bioavailability, pharmacokinetics, and analgesic activity of ketamine in humans. J Pharm Sci. 1982;71(5):539-542 (PMID 7097501): two-compartment disposition, terminal half-life 186 min, intramuscular bioavailability 93%, oral bioavailability 17% (extensive first-pass), analgesic plasma threshold ~160 ng/mL

mion_2013.title: Mion G, Villevieille T. Ketamine Pharmacology: An Update. CNS Neurosci Ther. 2013 (PMC6493357): two-compartment linear disposition, central volume ~70 L, steady-state Vd ~200-252 L (~2.3-3.6 L/kg), clearance ~79 L/h (12-20 mL/min/kg, hepatic-blood-flow limited), distribution half-life 11-16 min, elimination half-life 2-3 h, ~80% metabolised to norketamine (active, ~1/3 potency), norketamine half-life ~5 h

kamp_2020.title: Kamp J, Olofsen E, Henthorn TK, van Velzen M, Niesters M, Dahan A, et al. Pharmacokinetics of Ketamine and its Major Metabolites Norketamine, Hydroxynorketamine, and Dehydronorketamine: A Model-Based Analysis. Br J Anaesth. 2020;125(5):750-761 (S0007091220305717): norketamine formation clearance ~12.4 and elimination clearance ~13.5 L/h/70 kg

abuhelwa_2022.title: Abuhelwa AY, Somogyi AA, Loo CK, Glue P, Barratt DT, Foster DJR. Population Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics of the Therapeutic and Adverse Effects of Ketamine in Patients With Treatment-Refractory Depression. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2022 Sep;112(3):720-729 (PMID 35560226): 21 treatment-refractory depressed adults, 0.1-0.5 mg/kg single doses by i.v., s.c. and i.m.; two-compartment models with FIRST-ORDER absorption after s.c. and i.m.; i.m./s.c. bioavailability ~64% with indistinguishable absorption rate constants; CL 69.6 L/h/70kg, Vc 79.3 L/70kg, Vp 87.4 L/70kg.

ananyev_2026.title: Ananyev J, Myers TM. Intramuscular Ketamine Pharmacokinetics in Humans: A Review. Eur J Drug Metab Pharmacokinet. 2026;51:347-358 (doi 10.1007/s13318-026-01012-2): ten studies of IM ketamine PK; Cmax rises linearly from 27 ng/mL at 0.1 mg/kg to 1970 ng/mL at 6.0 mg/kg (~297 ng/mL at 1.0 mg/kg, ~15 min); Tmax 10-30 min and largely uncorrelated with dose; adult absorption rate constant 0.109 1/min; reported bioavailability 41.1-93.0%.

hornik_2018.title: Hornik CP, Gonzalez D, van den Anker J, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics of Intramuscular and Intravenous Ketamine in Children. J Clin Pharmacol. 2018 (PMID 29677389): 113 children (50 i.m., 63 i.v.); 'a 2-compartment model with first-order absorption following intramuscular administration and first-order elimination described the data best'; ka 2.51 /h; IM bioavailability 41%. Cited for the ABSORPTION INPUT TYPE only - its paediatric/critically-ill parameters are not used here.

metabolism: Ketamine is N-demethylated by the liver to norketamine, an active metabolite with about one-third of ketamine's potency. Because norketamine is cleared more slowly than the parent it accumulates and prolongs the effect. After oral dosing this first-pass conversion is so extensive that norketamine dominates — which is why this model offers only the IV/IM routes.
notes:
Ketamine - a dissociative anaesthetic (Ketalar, FDA-approved 1970) that is also a major recreational/club drug ('Special K', Schedule III) and, as esketamine, an antidepressant. Modelled as the diazepam template: a genuine TWO-COMPARTMENT parent (fast distribution then a slower terminal) plus a drawn ACTIVE metabolite (norketamine). This is the metabolite centerpiece of the illicit-drugs slate and one of the cleaner parent->metabolite pairs in the whole set.

WHY KETAMINE (and why on IV/IM, not oral). Ketamine is LINEAR over the clinical range (the FDA label and popPK models use a two-compartment LINEAR disposition; clearance is high and hepatic-blood-flow-limited, so mildly flow-dependent at extremes but dose-proportional in range - the propofol documented-linearity posture). Crucially the metabolite is only representable on the INTRAVENOUS/INTRAMUSCULAR route: oral ketamine has huge hepatic first-pass (F ~17%, Clements 1982), so orally most norketamine forms PRE-SYSTEMICALLY (the oseltamivir disqualifier) - a systemic-formation engine could not represent it. On IV/IM (Ketalar's real clinical route, and a real recreational route) there is no first-pass and norketamine forms from systemic ketamine, so the pair is valid and is drawn. Oral is therefore intentionally omitted (the diazepam posture - no route offered where the model would be wrong).

DISPOSITION2C - what is sourced vs derived. As with diazepam, Q and Vp are not reported directly, so only CL and Vc are read from sources and Q, Vp are derived offline from four macro-observables: CL ~1.13 L/h/kg (Mion 2013, ~79 L/h at 70 kg), Vc ~1.0 L/kg (~70 L, Mion 2013 - NOT the FDA label; see the centralVd conditions), Vss ~2.86 L/kg (Clements/Mion, ~200 L) and terminal beta t1/2 2.5 h. Working per-kg: k10 = CL/Vc = 1.13/h; beta = ln2/2.5 = 0.2773/h; Vp = Vss - Vc = 1.86 L/kg fixes k12/k21 = Vp/Vc = 1.86; solving alpha.beta = k10.k21 and alpha+beta = k10+k12+k21 gives k21 = 0.702/h, k12 = 1.305/h; Q = k12.Vc = 1.305 L/h/kg. The engine round-trips these back to alpha t1/2 ~14.5 min (the FDA label does say 10-15 min - half-lives are the one thing it does state) and beta t1/2 2.5 h, and Vc + Vp = 2.86 L/kg reproduces Vss. As for every 2-comp compound, Vc is the softest input and sets the distribution-peak amplitude C(0) = Dose/Vc, so the early spike height is somewhat illustrative while the terminal phase is well grounded.

METABOLITE (norketamine). fm ~80% is a single directly-citable number (~80% N-demethylated to norketamine, FDA label) - much cleaner than cocaine's or procainamide's soft/bimodal fm. Norketamine is active (~1/3 potency), and because its own half-life (~5 h) exceeds the parent's terminal (~2.5 h) it is elimination-rate-limited - it accumulates and outlives ketamine, the classic active-long-lived-metabolite story (compare oxypurinol). Its Vd is derived from CLM/t1/2_m (Kamp 2020), making exposure clearance-defined (AUC_m = fm.Dose/CL_m); it is not independently measured. The norketamine line is drawn on IV bolus, IV infusion and IM (the metabolite gate is the ENGINE route iv_bolus || oral || iv_infusion, and IM resolves onto oral). On IM, formation is systemic exactly as on IV - no pre-systemic term rides an injection, and derive.ts's appliesFirstPass enforces that for any future compound that does carry an ffp.

ROUTES. IV bolus (induction/recreational), IV infusion (analgesia, antidepressant, TCI) and INTRAMUSCULAR are all genuinely available clinical routes. No oral (pre-systemic metabolite formation - above).

IM (added 2026-07-20, the handoff section 12 'more routes' seam). Until this pass the file's own prose promised IV/IM and shipped IV only; IM is now real. It passes the input-type screen on row 2 (first-order from a depot, IV-like F, no first pass) with three independently opened sources agreeing on the input TYPE, which is the fact the screen turns on: Clements 1982 ('two-compartment open model', absorption after IM 'rapid'), Hornik 2018 ('a 2-compartment model with first-order absorption following intramuscular administration ... described the data best') and Abuhelwa 2022 ('two-compartment models with first-order absorption after s.c. and i.m.'). No engine math was added - engineRouteOf maps im onto the engine's first-order 'oral' input exactly as transdermal maps onto 'iv_infusion'. What IM is FOR pedagogically is the F contrast: same drug, same disposition, ~64% by needle against ~17% by mouth, and the gap is first pass.

WHY NOT SC, though Abuhelwa reports IM and SC together with 'indistinguishable first-order absorption rate constants' and a combined 64% F: that is evidence the two share an input SHAPE, not a licence to write a route whose own numbers were never separated out. A subcutaneous route gets added when a source reports it alone. Racemic ketamine is modelled; esketamine (Spravato, the S-enantiomer nasal antidepressant) is a separate product not modelled here. NMDA-antagonist mechanism; emergence phenomena are a well-known effect but concentration is not effect. Magnitude check (IM, and it is the first one this compound's Vc has ever faced - see below): a 100 mg IV bolus into Vc ~70 L gives C(0) = 1.43 mcg/mL, redistributing fast (alpha t1/2 ~15 min); the drawn norketamine line rises to ~0.44 mcg/mL and outlasts the parent (the modelled metabolite peak is ~3.3 h - later than the label's ~30 min; see the norketamine timing caveat above). EDUCATIONAL ILLUSTRATION ONLY - not for dosing.

WHAT IM EXPOSED, AND WHAT IS LEFT STANDING. Every route this compound offered before IM peaked somewhere unobservable: an IV bolus peaks at C(0), which no assay catches, and an infusion peaks at a rate-and-duration the user picks. IM is the first route whose peak is a real, reported, independently measured number, so it is the first test Vc has ever had to pass - and Vc is, by this file's own long-standing admission, its softest input. It comes out ~1.1x high across 0.5-6.0 mg/kg (details in routes.im.F). That is left as-is rather than tuned: the fix would be to re-derive the whole 2-comp micro-parameter set against Abuhelwa's adult Vc of 79.3 L/70kg, which would move the SHIPPED IV curves and needs its own pass and its own magnitude checks. Recorded here so the next curator finds a known, bounded, deliberate residual rather than rediscovering it as a surprise.
